records.ety: [L. defraudare; de- + fraudare to cheat, fr. fraus, fraudis, fraud: cf. OF. defrauder. See Fraud.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Defrauded; p. pr. & vb. n. Defrauding.]

defs: To deprive of some right, interest, or property, by a deceitful device; to withhold from wrongfully; to injure by embezzlement; to cheat; to overreach; as, to defraud a servant, or a creditor, or the state; -- with of before the thing taken or withheld.
quotes: Churches seem injured and defrauded of their rights.
